targetArea: VCPChat renderer UI / Codex Router visible results
issueReported: When Codex Router mode was enabled, the user could not see the information/reply produced by the routed task.

preImplementationObservation.appDataCodexRouterRecentFiles: No new AppData/codexRouter runtime artifact after the recent UI send.
preImplementationObservation.chatHistoryRecentWrites: The visible chat history file had recent ordinary VCPChat messages but no Codex Router result summary from the toggle path.

behavior: Router-mode and prefixed Router sends now render the user's task into the active chat.
A transient Codex Router thinking message is shown while the host run is in flight.
On success, the transient thinking message is replaced by an assistant message named Codex Router.

The assistant message contains a sanitized Chinese Markdown summary: status, policy version, model, execution profile, tool access, approval requirement, task id, planned primitives, executed steps, and blocking reasons when present.
On failure, a Codex Router failure message is rendered in the chat.
Visible non-thinking Router messages are appended to currentChatHistory and saved to the current topic history file, making them monitorable from AppData/UserData/.../history.json.
implemented.secretSafety: The chat summary intentionally avoids raw execution output and stores only governance/result metadata.

notValidated: No automated Electron send/click test was run.
User should send a new Codex Router task from the live window; after that, history.json can be inspected for the Codex Router summary.
